scads 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Quick Tutorial on scads

    Run S-LDSC Pipeline (hg38) with a Custom Continuous BED + Baseline v2.2

    run_sldsc_cont.Rd

    This function runs the S-LDSC pipeline using PolyFun scripts for:

    1. Munging summary statistics ("munge_polyfun_sumstats.py"),

    2. Generating continuous annotations from a user-supplied BED file (hg38) via an R function create_continuous_annotation,

    3. Computing LD scores (ldsc.py --l2),

    4. Performing final heritability estimation (ldsc.py --h2) by combining the custom annotation(s) with baseline v2.2 annotations.

    run_sldsc_cont(
  polyfun_path,
  ldsc_path,
  sumstats_path,
  n,
  trait,
  onekg_path,
  bed_dir,
  baseline_dir,
  frqfile_pref,
  hm3_snps,
  out_dir
)

    Arguments

    polyfun_path

    A character string specifying the directory containing the PolyFun scripts (e.g., ldsc.py, munge_polyfun_sumstats.py).

    ldsc_path

    A character string specifying the directory containing the ldsc.py script (not actually used here for annotation, but you may store related LDSC scripts in the same place).

    sumstats_path

    A character string specifying the directory with the cleaned summary statistics file <trait>_sumstats.txt.gz.

    n

    Integer. The number of samples in the GWAS.

    trait

    A character string representing the GWAS trait (e.g., "SIM").

    onekg_path

    A character string specifying the prefix to 1000 Genomes Plink files in hg38, e.g. "/path/1000G.EUR.hg38.". Each chromosome file is then 1000G.EUR.hg38.1.bed/.bim/.fam, etc.

    bed_dir

    A character string specifying the directory containing the user-supplied continuous BED file(s) in hg38. Only the first matching *.cont.bed found is used to create the annotation for each chromosome. (You can adjust pattern if you want a different naming scheme.)

    baseline_dir

    A character string specifying the prefix to baseline v2.2 annotations for LDSC, e.g. "/path/baselineLD_v2.2/baselineLD.".

    frqfile_pref

    A character string specifying the prefix to allele frequency files in hg38, used by LDSC (e.g. "/path/1000G.EUR.hg38.").

    hm3_snps

    A character string specifying the path to the HapMap3 SNP file (e.g. "hm3_no_MHC.list.txt").

    out_dir

    A character string specifying the output directory for LDSC results. The function will create:

    • "annotations/<trait>" for annotation files

    • "results" for final LDSC logs/results

    Value

    No object is returned. All intermediate files (<trait>.<chr>.annot.gz, <trait>.<chr>.l2.ldscore.gz, etc.) are written to out_dir/annotations/<trait>, and the final LDSC results (.log and .results files) to out_dir/results.

    Details

    Step-by-step:

    1. Munge summary stats <trait>_sumstats.txt.gz into a parquet file with munge_polyfun_sumstats.py.

    2. For each chromosome 1..22:

      • Create a continuous annotation (via create_continuous_annotation) from the single user-supplied BED file (found in bed_dir) plus the .bim for that chromosome.

      • Compute LD scores (ldsc.py --l2) for this annotation.

    3. Finally, run S-LDSC (ldsc.py --h2) referencing both the newly created annotation (out_dir/annotations/<trait>/<trait>.<chr>) and baseline_dir, providing frqfile_pref for the allele frequency files and the hm3_snps for SNP filtering.

    Examples

    if (FALSE) {
run_sldsc_cont(
  polyfun_path = "/path/polyfun",
  sumstats_path= "/path/sumstats",
  n            = 60000,
  trait        = "SIM",
  onekg_path   = "/path/1000G.EUR.hg38.",
  bed_dir      = "/path/my_bedfiles", # containing e.g. "k4_annotations_cont.bed"
  baseline_dir = "/path/baselineLD_v2.2/baselineLD.",
  frqfile_pref = "/path/1000G.EUR.hg38.",
  hm3_snps     = "/path/hm3_no_MHC.list.txt",
  out_dir      = "/path/k4_annotations_cont_results"
)
}
